$comment: Degenerate while loops whose body ALWAYS breaks — the loop has no back-edge, so it is not a loop in the CFG. Before the canonical StructCfMarker derivation (D-OPT4-1), the production verifier REJECTED these programs (I_StructCfMismatch: 'LoopHeader has no back-edge predecessor') because the lowering's creation-time LoopHeader stamp survived into a CFG the break had de-looped. The derivation re-stamps from the actual CFG (the while-header normalizes to a plain conditional), flipping a rejection into a working program: once(30)=35, +7 = exit 42. NOTE: the sibling do{...break;}while(cond) class additionally leaves an UNREACHABLE continueBB, which trips the separately-tracked pre-existing I_UnreachableBlock issue — deliberately NOT in this example.
